prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: A Casserole for the Missing
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Mother Was Right All Along
Tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
Theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.
Omen: bees in winter
Relationship dynamic: career vs family guilt
Props: unopened letter, ceramic owl, snow globe

Cast:
- Noah Bell | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=magazine editor | threat_aura=0.39 | space=storage unit described as donation overflow | secrets=has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Sienna Mercer | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=marina manager | threat_aura=0.32 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=knows where evidence should have been
- Jenna Lark | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=bartender who knows everybody's business | threat_aura=0.52 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for; once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
- Rachel Pike | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.73 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed; had brunch with someone officially deceased
- Daniel Pike | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=artisan developer | threat_aura=0.92 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=has a second phone labeled as a work precaution
- Jenna Bell | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.52 | space=storage unit described as seasonal materials | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it; keeps screenshots like religious texts
- Mara Winterson | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Elias Cross | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Sienna Mercer is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Noah Bell says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Sienna?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Mara. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Noah. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The unopened letter is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Rachel."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
